top_signals.title: Gold Steady as Uncertainty Clouds Talks to End US-Iran War
top_signals.summary: Gold was steady as renewed clashes in the Middle East cast doubt over negotiations between the US and Iran to end the war that’s upended energy flows and heightened inflation concerns.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: US and Iran exchange renewed fire as tensions rise over stalled peace talks
top_signals.summary: Iranian state-affiliated media has said Tehran is suspending negotiations in response to Israeli bombardment of Lebanon
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: What Hormuz Is Teaching Traders About Utilities
top_signals.summary: Oil traders have spent months obsessing over tanker movements, shipping insurance costs and the fate of crude cargoes attempting to navigate the Strait of Hormuz. But now, some of that attention will be shifting to a longer-term play: a potential restructuring of American utilities themselves.  Instead, Hormuz is exposing how vulnerable electricity markets remain to fuel price shocks, even after years of investment in renewable energy. While crude oil prices have dominated headlines, the effects of the disruption are steadily working their…
top_signals.why_it_matters: Commodity repricing impacting inflation expectations and industrial supply chains.
